Hosts up to 20 ROK Navy warships + 3 submarines + 2 cruise ships of up to 150,000 tons (per cruise pier dimensions).
_poi_meta.infamous_2017_incident: On March 11, 2017, Costa Serena docked at Jeju Port (north) with 3,400+ passengers, but ALL passengers refused to disembark — they remained on board for 4 hours before the ship departed for Tianjin. Eighty waiting tour buses, customs officers, and local guides went home empty. The vessel was a chartered exclusive Chinese-corporate trip from Fukuoka, Japan; the no-disembark protest was politically motivated.
_poi_meta.semi_homeporting_2025: Adora Magic City (Adora Cruises) began semi-homeporting at Gangjeong on May 1, 2025 — first roundtrip from Jeju. 33 roundtrips planned through end-2025, 5–6 day voyages connecting Shanghai ↔ Jeju ↔ Japanese ports, accommodating 60-120 Korean passengers per voyage.
nearby_attractions: Jungmun Tourist Complex (Jungmun-dong, Saekdal-dong, Daepo-dong; multiple resorts)
Cheonjeyeon Falls + Seonimgyo Bridge (~10 min drive)
Seogwipo old town + Olle Maeil Market (~10-15 min drive)
Cheonjiyeon Falls (~15 min drive, near downtown Seogwipo)

visitBasics.hours: Cruise terminal operates per ship arrival schedule
visitBasics.closed: No fixed closure — operates by ship schedule
visitBasics.admission: Free for transit
visitBasics.walking: Minimal — flat terminal interior; reportedly better wheelchair accessibility than the northern Jeju Port

convenience.restroom: Restrooms in arrival/departure halls
convenience.parking: 53-bus outdoor parking capacity (per VisitKorea); fills quickly when 2 ships arrive simultaneously

smartNotes.photo: Two cruise ships docking simultaneously (most common shot at this dual-berth port); Hallasan visible on clear days from terminal apron; coastal volcanic-rock shoreline beyond the breakwater (the historic Gureombi rock formation, blasted in 2012, is now under cement)
smartNotes.facilities: Tourist info center, convenience store, currency exchange, tax-refund counter, lounge — all standard cruise terminal services. Anti-base activists hold daily Catholic mass + 100-bow protests at the main gate (separate from cruise-passenger areas, away from terminal — cruise guests rarely encounter these)
smartNotes.tip: Allow 30 min between debarkation and bus boarding. Gangjeong is the more accessible terminal for mobility-impaired passengers. If your ship docks at Gangjeong rather than Jeju Port (north), your tour DEFINITELY needs a port-aware itinerary — driving north to Seongsan Ilchulbong is 70+ min one-way, eating the day. The southern itinerary (Hallasan 1100 + Jusangjeolli + Cheonjiyeon + Olle Market) works in a comfortable 8-hour window with all stops within 30 min of port.

_poi_meta.distinction_from_cheonjeyeon: CRITICAL: Cheonjiyeon Falls (천지연 폭포) is in downtown Seogwipo, near the Olle Market. Cheonjeyeon Falls (천제연 폭포 — three-tier waterfall in Jungmun) is a DIFFERENT waterfall ~15 min west. The two names sound nearly identical and are commonly confused.
_poi_meta.trail_surface: Flat paved + wooden bridge sections through subtropical garden
trail_features: Camphor trees, bamboo thickets, wild orchids, Psilotum nudum
Traditional Korean raft (테우 / teu) display near trail start
Bridge over the koi-fish pond before reaching the falls
Three piles of stacked rocks placed by locals praying for family health
Stone guardian (dol hareubang) statues at entry/exit
_poi_meta.natural_monument_no_eel: 27 (Mutae eel — the marbled eel Anguilla marmorata) — habitat protection. Some sources cite NM No. 258 also for this same eel within Cheonjiyeon. Check VisitJeju official for current NM number.
rare_flora_and_fauna: Mutae (무태) / marbled eel — Natural Monument No. 27 — nocturnal, illumination at night triggers their activity
Camphor trees (Cinnamomum camphora) — Natural Monument trees
Subtropical Psilotum nudum (primitive plant) on volcanic rock walls
_poi_meta.stream_source: Spring rising from the floor of Somban Stream (Yeonhee-chun)
_poi_meta.indoor_outdoor_note: Trail and falls are 100% outdoor. Coffee shops + 7-11 at the parking area provide indoor refuge for separate visits.
_poi_meta.famous_legend: Seven fairies serving the King of Heaven descended on cloud stairs to bathe in Cheonjiyeon's clear water — the name 'sky-meets-earth' commemorates this mythic descent.
_poi_meta.swimming_status: Strictly prohibited (eel habitat protection + Natural Monument designation)
_poi_meta.two_dam_system: Two small dams below the pool maintain water level for ecological + safety reasons
_poi_meta.famous_for: Night illumination — daily after sunset, the cliff face + bridge + cliff base are lit up; the only Jeju waterfall with regular night-time public viewing

visitBasics.hours: Daily 09:00–22:00 (last entry 21:30); night illumination begins ~sunset and runs to ~22:00
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round
visitBasics.admission: Adults ₩2,000 / Youth (7-24) ₩1,000 / Free for under 7 and 65+
visitBasics.walking: Easy — ~1 km flat paved + wooden bridge path through subtropical forest, ~15 min one way; some short stair sections at the falls viewing area

convenience.restroom: Public restrooms at the entrance/ticket area (none past the bridge)
convenience.parking: Free on-site parking lot at the entrance (Korean GPS phone: 7331528)

smartNotes.photo: Wooden bridge over the koi pond just before the falls (classic frame shot); falls + jade-green pond with cliff backdrop (signature composition); night illumination shots (long exposure recommended); the small circular staircase + protruding rocks for the iconic close-up (CAUTION — slippery when wet, no safety fence); arched seven-nymph bridge sculpture at the entrance
smartNotes.facilities: Park area called Chilsipni Park; 7-11 convenience store at parking area; coffee shop + Korean food shop near entrance; small temple (Hwangnyong) accessible up stairs from the coffee shop; grilled-squid stalls in the park; seating areas + benches
smartNotes.tip: DO NOT confuse with Cheonjeyeon Falls (Three-Tier Falls in Jungmun) — different waterfall ~15 min west. **Best at night** for the daily illumination — Mutae eels (active at night) emerge from the pool. Slippery rocks at the base — wear shoes with grip. Pair naturally with Jeongbang Falls (6-min drive — the only waterfall in Asia falling directly into ocean) and Olle Maeil Market (10-min walk) for a Seogwipo old-town afternoon. Allow 40-60 min for a regular visit; +15 min for the night illumination; +90 min if photographing with a tripod.

_poi_meta.ranking: Largest and oldest permanent market in Seogwipo (south Jeju)
_poi_meta.comparison_with_jeju_city: Smaller and more traditional than Dongmun Traditional Market (Jeju City — north, larger and flashier); Seogwipo Maeil Olle has a more 'local + traditional' feel per multiple food bloggers
specialties: Hallabong (한라봉) tangerines — fresh fruit + processed snacks
Black pork (heuk-dwaeji) dishes — steamed buns, dumplings, jerky, cutlet rolls
Tangerine snacks — Hallabong soft-serve, tangerine hotteok, tangerine steamed bread, hallabong-flavored macarons
Live seafood + sashimi (red tilefish, silver cutlassfish)
Omegi-tteok (오메기떡) — traditional Jeju rice cake with red bean
Black pork jerky in Jeju gamgyul (tangerine), cheongyang red pepper, sweet almond, garlic flavors
Seogwipo gourmet buns at Jeseong Bakery (octopus-shaped buns, butter red bean, green onion cream cheese, black pork octopus)
famous_food_stalls: Jeseong Bakery (제송제과) — octopus-shaped black pork buns + chive egg + fire-spicy pork + cheese & black pork buns (most-photographed market stall)
Four Streets Olle (네거리올레) — kid-friendly kimbap rolls
Tangerine Hotteok (귤호떡) — most popular sweet stall
Hallasan Black Pork Dumplings (한라산 흑돼지 만두) — crispy outside, savory black pork inside
Black Pork Cutlet Roll (흑돼지 고사리 롤카츠) — crispy + filling
Hallabong Soft-Serve — topped with dried Hallabong slice

olle_night_market.name: Seogwipo Olle Night Market
olle_night_market.specialty: Grilled eel, king crab, black pork steak, black pork yakisoba — only Seogwipo night market

_poi_meta.transit_to_falls: 10-min walk to Cheonjiyeon Falls; 15-min drive to Jeongbang Falls
near: Lee Jung-seop Street (5-min walk, named after the celebrated Korean painter)
Lee Jung-seop Culture Street + Art District
_poi_meta.indoor_outdoor_note: Market is fully roofed/covered (indoor traditional market); designated covered shopping arcades. Some stalls and the parking lot edges are outdoor. Counted as 0.85 indoor for time-weighting since the dominant experience is walking covered alleys.
_poi_meta.atmosphere: Vibrant covered market, local-meets-tourist mix, designed seating areas along shops to eat purchased food on-site
_poi_meta.operating_status: Year-round; many stalls close ~17:00 then night market opens around the corner
_poi_meta.evolution: Past: produce + seafood + manufactured goods + clothing. Recent shift to local specialties (tangerines + Hallabong + fish + black pork) catering to tourist majority

visitBasics.hours: Summer 07:00–21:00 / Winter 07:00–20:00 (most shops); Olle Night Market opens ~17:00
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round
visitBasics.admission: Free entry; food and shopping per-item
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat covered alleys (mostly indoor); seating areas along shops

convenience.restroom: Public restrooms within the market and at nearby Seogwipo City Hall
convenience.parking: Multiple multi-story public car parks nearby (paid, can be crowded peak hours)

smartNotes.photo: Octopus-shaped black pork buns at Jeseong Bakery (signature shot — the buns look like dumplings but are bread-textured); Hallabong soft-serve with dried Hallabong slice on top; seafood aquarium tanks at the live-fish stalls; tangerine pyramids at the produce vendors; dol hareubang (stone grandfather) souvenirs
smartNotes.facilities: 200+ shops, 140+ street vendors, ~20 night-market food stalls, designated seating areas to eat-on-site, souvenir stalls (tangerine-shaped hats, hallabong jam, citrus oil/soap, candles, chocolate, juices), cooking class venues nearby (tangerine macaron / black pork classes)
smartNotes.tip: Skip the Hallabong ORANGE ICE CREAM (per multiple bloggers — doesn't taste like orange, mostly vanilla). The HALLABONG SOFT-SERVE topped with a dried Hallabong slice is the genuine version. Octopus-shaped buns at Jeseong Bakery have a long line on weekends — line moves fast, worth the wait. Best for cruise day stops: covered + flat + central + 10-min walk to Cheonjiyeon Falls = perfect 30-45 min wander or 60-90 min for a meal. Prices on souvenir items can be slightly higher than Dongmun Market in Jeju City. Shops generally close ~17:00; if you want night market, plan for 17:30+ visit. Cash preferred at smaller stalls; bigger restaurants take card.

verified_facts.geography: Largest of Jeju's offshore islands, ~3.8 km off Jeju's east coast. Volcanic plateau formed by lava eruption. Named 'Cow Island' because the silhouette resembles a reclining cow when viewed from the sea.
verified_facts.size_population: Approximately 6 km² with about 370 residents. Highest point is Udobong (also called Mam Oreum) at 132 m, topped by a lighthouse and panoramic viewpoint.
verified_facts.ferry: 15-minute crossing from Seongsan Port (most frequent — every ~30 minutes during peak hours, 15-20 sailings daily) to either Cheonjin Port or Haumokdong Port on Udo. Tickets purchased same-day at port window with ID/passport. Last return ferry around 17:00-18:00 (varies by season).
verified_facts.fare: Round-trip approximately 8,900 KRW adults / 4,900 KRW children (current 2025-2026 rates per Klook and Visit Korea).
verified_facts.vehicle_restrictions: Rental-car loading is restricted (overnight-stay accommodation booking required, plus other conditions). Day-trip visitors are directed to the island's shuttle bus, scooter, e-bike, or pink mini-car rentals.
verified_facts.scenic_attractions: Udo Palgyeong (Eight Scenic Views) including Hagosudong Beach (white coral sand), Geommeolle Beach (black volcanic sand), Seobinbaeksa, and Seondol rock formation.
verified_facts.specialties: Peanut farming heritage — Udo peanut ice cream is the signature snack, also peanut beer and Hallabong (mandarin) ade.
verified_facts.popularity: Welcomed about 2.23 million visitors in 2016, remains one of Jeju's top day-trip destinations.

visitBasics.hours: Ferry operations roughly 07:30/08:00 to 17:00/18:00 (varies by season). Island accessible during ferry hours.
visitBasics.closed: Ferry may be canceled for weather (high winds, fog, typhoon)
visitBasics.admission: Free island access. Round-trip ferry ~8,900 KRW adults
visitBasics.walking: Easy on shuttle bus loop; moderate if biking or hiking the coastal path

convenience.restroom: At ferry terminals and main beaches
convenience.parking: Ample parking at Seongsan Port (mainland side) for visitors leaving cars on Jeju

smartNotes.photo: Hagosudong's white coral sand against blue water is the signature shot; Udobong lighthouse for panoramic island views; Seopjikoji and Seongsan Ilchulbong both visible from the ferry.
smartNotes.facilities: Coffee shops and peanut-ice-cream stalls cluster near Cheonjin Port and around Hagosudong Beach. Restaurants tend to close after the last ferry departs.
smartNotes.tip: Time the visit so you board no later than the second-to-last ferry back; missing the last ferry forces an unplanned overnight. The shuttle bus (~15-min intervals) is the easiest option for visitors without scooter licenses.

manjanggul_lava_tube.name: Manjanggul Lava Tube

verified_facts.scale: Total length about 7.4 km (some classifications include connected tubes up to ~13-15 km). Main tunnel width 18 m, height 23-25 m — among the largest lava tubes in the world. Three entrances exist due to ceiling collapse; only Entrance #2 is open to visitors.
verified_facts.tourist_section: Open passage is about 1 km (visitors walk in and back the same way, ~30-45 min round trip).
verified_facts.lava_column: The 7.6-meter-tall lava column at the end of the tourist section is recognized as the world's tallest lava column.
verified_facts.geology: Part of the Geomunoreum Lava Tube System. Formed by lava that erupted from Geomunoreum, a parasite cone of Hallasan, between the late Pliocene and Pleistocene; the most recent dating revisions estimate ~8,000 years rather than the older 100,000+ year figure. Connects to Gimnyeongsa Cave before the passage was blocked by collapse.
verified_facts.unesco: Inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in 2007 as part of 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' (along with Seongsan Ilchulbong and Mount Halla). Also designated Natural Monument of Korea.
verified_facts.interior_environment: Year-round interior temperature around 11-21°C (most reliably 11-13°C deep in the cave). Extremely cool relief in summer, mild compared to outside in winter. Path is dark and damp.
verified_facts.operations_status_2026: IMPORTANT: The cave was closed for major facility maintenance from December 29, 2023 following a rockfall, with the official Visit Jeju notice stating completion 'scheduled by March 31, 2026' followed by additional inspections before reopening. As of April 2026 the reopening status should be re-confirmed before scheduling a visit.

visitBasics.hours: When open: typically 09:00-18:00, last entry 17:10
visitBasics.closed: Closed first Wednesday of each month (when operating); may close in heavy rain. Verify reopening status post-March 2026.
visitBasics.admission: Adults 4,000 KRW (group 3,000) / Teens-Children 2,000 KRW (group 1,500)
visitBasics.walking: Easy-to-moderate flat 1 km in/1 km out on damp uneven floor; sturdy shoes recommended

convenience.restroom: At the visitor center near the entrance
convenience.parking: Free parking lot at the entrance

smartNotes.photo: The 7.6 m world's-largest lava column at the end of the tourist section is the iconic shot; the long perspective shots down the tunnel show the 18×23 m scale.
smartNotes.facilities: Information center near the parking lot explains lava-tube formation before entering — strongly worth 10 minutes for context. No food inside the cave.
smartNotes.tip: Bring a light jacket even in summer (interior 11-13°C). Confirm reopening status before driving out — the cave was closed for multi-year maintenance through early 2026.

sources: UNESCO Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes (whc.unesco.org)

verified_facts.geology: 395 m trachytic lava dome on Jeju's southwest coast. Formed by viscous lava that piled up rather than flowing outward, giving it a distinctive bell-like dome shape (not a typical volcanic cone). Estimated 700,000-800,000 years old.
verified_facts.name_meaning: Sanbangsan literally means 'mountain with a cave', referring to Sanbanggul, a tafoni-style cave at ~145-150 m elevation on the south face containing the Sanbanggulsa cave temple.
verified_facts.cave_temple: Sanbanggulsa is approximately 10 m × 5 m × 5 m. Used as a Buddhist temple at least since the Goryeo period (918-1392); Joseon-era officials' names are carved into the cave's rocks. Water dripping from the ceiling is locally said to be the tears of Sanbangdeok, the goddess who protects the mountain.
verified_facts.geopark_status: Part of the Jeju Island UNESCO Global Geopark, alongside Hallasan and Seongsan Ilchulbong. The cliffs on the southern face show columnar jointing and weathering pits up to 200 m tall.
verified_facts.natural_monument: About 247,935 m² of cliff-wall vegetation was designated a Natural Monument of Jeju Province in 1993.
verified_facts.base_temples: Bomunsa Temple, Sanbangsa Temple, and Gwangmyeongsa Temple cluster at the foot. Spring rapeseed (mustard) flower fields create the photogenic golden carpet around the base.
verified_facts.summit: The 395 m peak itself is closed to climbing for conservation; visitors stop at the cave temple ~150 m up. Round-trip climb to the cave is about 0.6 mi / 1 km, 27-60 min, with stairs.

visitBasics.hours: Summer 09:00-18:00, Winter 09:00-17:30 (subject to weather)
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round (weather may close paths)
visitBasics.admission: Approximately 1,000 KRW for the cave temple (some sources note up to 2,500 KRW for combined-area tickets including Yongmeori)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — stairs up to ~150 m elevation, ~278 ft elevation gain

convenience.restroom: At the base parking area
convenience.parking: Public and private lots at the base

smartNotes.photo: Spring rapeseed flowers against the bell-shaped lava dome (March-April) is the iconic Sanbangsan shot; the ocean view from the cave temple at 150 m elevation is the second-best.
smartNotes.facilities: Yongmeori Coast walk and Hamel Ship Exhibition Centre are both within 5-10 minutes — common pairing.
smartNotes.tip: The peak is often clouded due to elevation+ocean proximity; clear-weather mornings are best. No photography pointed toward the main altar inside the cave temple — be respectful as it remains an active prayer site.

verified_facts.dimensions: Wide flat crater: ~650 m diameter (some sources cite ~350 m as the rim-to-rim minimum), ~100 m deep, ~2,070 m circumference. From above it resembles a circular stadium.
verified_facts.geology: Formed by phreatomagmatic eruption (magma + groundwater interaction) — unusual among Jeju's ~360 oreum cones, most of which are scoria cones. Spewed minimal lava, leaving almost no cone, just the stadium-like depression.
verified_facts.vegetation: Inside the crater: north slope hosts warm-temperate vegetation (red-thorn trees, magnolias, rare winter strawberries); south slope hosts cool-temperate vegetation (evergreens, maples, mountain strawberries). The unusual concentration of biodiversity in such a small space attracts research interest.
verified_facts.iconic_season: Most photographed October-November when the silvergrass (Miscanthus sinensis) covering the surrounding plain turns golden — 'silver waves' is the Korean nickname.
verified_facts.access: Walking up to the rim takes only a few minutes from the parking lot on a paved path; full circuit including the silvergrass plains takes ~40-60 min.

visitBasics.hours: Mar-Jun, Sep-Oct 09:00-18:40; Nov-Feb, Jul-Aug 09:00-17:40 (last admission 40 min before closing)
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round; may close in inclement weather
visitBasics.admission: Adults 7,000 KRW / Teens-Children 6,000 KRW (group 6,000 / 5,000 for 20+ people)
visitBasics.walking: Easy — paved short path to the crater rim, optional silvergrass-plain loop

convenience.restroom: At the entrance/parking area
convenience.parking: Free parking on site

smartNotes.photo: October-November silvergrass at golden-hour is the bucket-list shot. The crater itself is fully vegetated (no bare lava), so visitors expecting a Mars-like crater interior are sometimes disappointed — manage expectations.
smartNotes.facilities: Free explanation program (Wed-Mon) at 09:30, 10:30, 14:00, 15:00, 16:00 — good for first-time visitors curious about phreatomagmatic geology.
smartNotes.tip: Pair with nearby Eco Land Theme Park, Bijarim Forest, or Gimnyeong Maze Park to fill out an east-Jeju half-day. Avoid in heavy rain — the silvergrass mats and the rim is exposed.

verified_facts.scale: Considered the largest Buddhist temple complex in East Asia. Site spans 122,100 m². Main hall (Daejeokgwangjeon) is 30 m tall and ~3,305 m² in floor area. Architecture follows early-Joseon Buddhist temple style though the temple itself is recently built.
verified_facts.main_buddha: 5 m-tall Vairocana (Celestial Buddha) statue on a 4 m pedestal — the tallest such statue in Korea, made of wood — flanked by two smaller Buddha statues. Walls feature large altar portraits sculpted in detail.
verified_facts.bell: Giant Buddhist temple bell weighing 18 tons stands in front of the Dharma Hall.
verified_facts.buddha_count: The main hall houses thousands of small golden Buddha statues — some sources cite 18,000+, others 80,000 (figure varies; the visual impression of rows of small Buddhas across multiple floors is consistent).
verified_facts.construction_history: Built on the site of a small hermitage called Yaksuam ('hermitage of medicinal water') where a perennial mineral spring flows. Construction began in 1981/1982 by the monk Hyein, completed in 1996. Name means 'temple where medicinal water flows'.
verified_facts.olle_trail: Junction point between Jeju Olle Trail Route 7 (start) and Route 8 (end) — visited by Olle hikers as well as tourists.
verified_facts.templestay: Active Templestay site since January 9, 2006. Programs: 1-day 40,000 KRW; 2-day Experience 70,000; 2-day Relax 50,000.
verified_facts.additional_features: Includes a small natural cave-temple (Gulbeopdang) just behind the main hall, a Hall of 500 Arhans, a Samseonggak shrine, a Sarira Pagoda for relics, and 3-story dormitory.

visitBasics.hours: Daily, generally 06:00-21:00 (no fixed gate hours; open as a working temple)
visitBasics.closed: Open daily; some halls may restrict during ceremonies
visitBasics.admission: Free entry (donations welcome). Templestay programs paid separately.
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat parking-lot to gate then stairs to the main hall; short additional climbs to side halls

convenience.restroom: On site
convenience.parking: Free parking

smartNotes.photo: The dramatic stair approach with the main hall framed at the top is the iconic shot. From the second/third floor balconies the Pacific Ocean is visible behind the temple grounds.
smartNotes.facilities: Vegetarian temple meals available during Templestay; tangerines from the temple courtyard can be picked in season.
smartNotes.tip: No photography pointed at the main altar inside; be respectful as the temple is fully active. Pair with Daepo Jusangjeolli Cliff (15 min away) or Jungmun beach area.

verified_facts.scale: Approximately 330,000 m² (some sources say 100,000 m² for the lighting-festival area, 30,000 m² for core garden — official Visit Korea cites the 330,000 m² total). About 5,000 plant species cultivated.
verified_facts.founded: Conceived in 1992; land purchased 1994; opened to the public in 1996. Created and personally designed by Han Sang-kyung (also written Han San-kyung) of Sahmyook University, who at one point sold his house to fund the project.
verified_facts.name_origin: Named after Korea's longstanding nickname 'Land of the Morning Calm' (Joseon).
verified_facts.lighting_festival: Hosts Korea's first natural-environment light festival, running every winter — 2025-2026 edition: December 5, 2025 to March 15, 2026. More than 30,000 LED displays cover ~330,000 m². Eco-friendly LEDs.
verified_facts.festival_signature_areas: Hakyung Garden, Hometown House Garden, Bonsai Garden, Moonlight Garden, Sky Path, Garden of Eden. 'The Happiness Tunnel', 'Forest Tunnel', and 'Light Road' are signature lit corridors.
verified_facts.festival_hours: Festival period Sun-Fri 11:00-21:00, Sat 11:00-23:00. Outside festival period 08:30-19:00 (last entry 18:00).
verified_facts.transit: From Cheongpyeong Station (Gyeongchun Line), bus 30-6 or 30-7 (1,550 KRW, 32 min) or 15-min taxi (~16,000 KRW). Gapyeong City Tour Bus also routes here.

visitBasics.hours: Spring-Autumn 08:30-19:00 (last entry 18:00); Winter Festival Sun-Fri 11:00-21:00, Sat 11:00-23:00
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round
visitBasics.admission: Adults 11,000 KRW / Teens 8,500 KRW / Children 7,500 KRW (pricing 2025; ~2,000 KRW discount on adjacent zoo for ticket holders)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — extensive paths over rolling terrain; budget 1.5-2 hours for daytime visit, 2-3 hours for full lighting festival

convenience.restroom: Multiple, signposted
convenience.parking: Large free parking; lockers available (1,500-2,000 KRW, 500-KRW coins only)

smartNotes.photo: Spring (cherry blossoms), autumn (foliage), and winter lighting festival (post-sunset) all photograph differently. The light tunnels at dusk are the festival signature.
smartNotes.facilities: On-site restaurants and cafes operate to about 20:00; Food Garden (Korean comfort food) and Good Morning Coffee (hanok roasting house) are the main meal stops.
smartNotes.tip: For the lighting festival, arrive ~16:00 to see the garden in daylight, then stay through illumination (~17:00 onward in winter). Heavy snow February is the most magical scene.

verified_facts.concept: French cultural theme village built around the concept 'flowers, stars, and the Little Prince' (Saint-Exupéry's 1943 novel Le Petit Prince). 16 French-style buildings host galleries, exhibits, cafés, music-box hall, and a marionette theater.
verified_facts.founded: Opened July 2008 by founder Han Hong-seop. Located in the mountains of Gapyeong about 25 minutes from Nami Island.
verified_facts.key_attractions: Saint-Exupéry memorial hall; Orgel House (200-year-old music box collection); French housing exhibition; gallery of le coq gaulois (Gallic rooster) sculptures and paintings; marionette theater with daily performances; Little Prince exhibit and statues throughout.
verified_facts.k_drama_legacy: Filming location for many K-dramas: Beethoven Virus (2008), Personal Taste (2010), Secret Garden (2010), My Love from the Star (2013-2014), Bon Appétit Your Majesty (2025 — Maison de Marie doubled as Im Yoon-ah's character's room), and the variety show Running Man.
verified_facts.adjacent_attraction: Pinocchio & Da Vinci Italian Village is directly adjacent — combo tickets available (Italian Village only 12,000 KRW; both villages 19,500 KRW adults).
verified_facts.size_caveat: Compact site — typical visit 1.5-2 hours. Visitors expecting a large attraction often note the modest scale; visitors there for K-drama spotting and photo ops generally satisfied.
verified_facts.official_recognition: Selected as part of the 2026 Korea-France 140th Anniversary Commemorative Program.

visitBasics.hours: 09:00-18:00 (last entry 17:00); some Mondays closed in off-season; night-festival hours announced separately
visitBasics.closed: Some Mondays in off-season
visitBasics.admission: Adults 12,000 KRW / Teens 8,500 / Children 7,500 (online discounts available; combined Petite France + Italian Village 19,500 adults)
visitBasics.walking: Easy-to-moderate — gentle slopes and stairs between the 16 buildings

convenience.restroom: On site
convenience.parking: Free parking

smartNotes.photo: Pastel-colored timber-framed houses against the green mountain backdrop are the signature shots. The viewing deck at the top offers the best wide composition. K-drama scene-spotting is a guided activity for fans.
smartNotes.facilities: Snack stalls only — waffles, dumplings, fish-cake soup, coffee. Most visitors eat at adjacent Italian Village or in nearby Cheongpyeong instead.
smartNotes.tip: Best paired with Nami Island and/or Garden of Morning Calm via the Gapyeong City Tour Bus to fill a full day. Late afternoon arrival lets you see daytime + early-evening illumination.

verified_facts.ownership_history: South Korea's largest theme park. Opened 1976 as 'Yongin Jayeon Nongwon' (Yongin Natural Farm). Renamed 'Everland' in 1996 for its 20th anniversary. Operated by Samsung C&T Corporation, a Samsung Group affiliate.
verified_facts.scale: Annual attendance approximately 5.85-6.6 million visitors (ranked 19th globally for amusement-park attendance in 2018). Site occupies the Everland Resort complex of theme park + zoo + Caribbean Bay water park.
verified_facts.themed_zones: Five themed areas: Global Fair (entrance, world-landmark replicas), American Adventure (1950s-Americana with Rocksville street), Magic Land (rides + Thunder Falls), Zootopia (15,000 m² with 2,000 animals across ~200 species), European Adventure (T Express + alpine/Mediterranean theming).
verified_facts.t_express: T Express — Korea's first wooden roller coaster, opened March 2008, built by Intamin with Rocky Mountain Construction. 1.64 km track, 46 m height, 77° drop angle (one of the steepest wooden coasters in the world), 104 km/h top speed, 3.12 min ride duration.
verified_facts.pandas: Aibao and Lebao (giant pandas gifted by China) arrived 2016. Fubao — first giant panda born in South Korea — born to Aibao in 2020. Twin daughters Ruibao and Huibao born 2023.
verified_facts.caribbean_bay: Adjacent water park, opened 1996 — at the time of opening, the world's largest indoor/outdoor combined water park. Reached 1 million visitors 13 months after opening.
verified_facts.festivals: Year-round festivals: Tulip Festival (Mar-Apr), Rose Festival (May-Jun, since 1986 — claimed Korea's first flower festival), Summer Splash, Halloween Festival, Christmas Fantasy / Romantic Illumination.

visitBasics.hours: Variable: typically 10:00-22:00 weekdays, 09:30-22:00 weekends/holidays. Confirm same-day on official site/app.
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round
visitBasics.admission: 1-Day Ticket: Adult 54,000-64,000 KRW / Child 43,000-51,000 KRW (depending on season; 20% foreigner discount at gate; Klook discounts often available)
visitBasics.walking: High — large park with hilly terrain; full visit easily 8-10 km on foot

convenience.restroom: Throughout park
convenience.parking: Paid parking lot, large capacity

smartNotes.photo: T Express drop and the Tulip Festival 'Romantic Tower Tree' (26 m flower sculpture with LEDs) are the signatures. Aesop's Umbrella Alley is a peak Instagram spot.
smartNotes.facilities: Smart Queue (free, in-app reservation system) is essential to ride T Express, Lost Valley, Safari World, Panda World, etc., before 14:00 — register the moment you enter the park.
smartNotes.tip: From central Seoul: Bus 5002A/B from Gangnam Station Exit 4/5 (~35 min) is the most direct. By rail: Bundang Line to Giheung, transfer to Yongin EverLine, exit at Jeondae-Everland Station, free shuttle bus to gate. Avoid mid-July to mid-August (peak crowd + heat) and Christmas/New Year (extreme queues).

verified_facts.scale_significance: Korea's first open-air museum of traditional folk culture. Approximately 991,735 m² (245 acres / 300,000 pyeong) of natural environment.
verified_facts.houses: Over 260 traditional houses and structures reproduced from the late Joseon Dynasty, representing different regions and social classes (commoner farmhouses through yangban scholar-class residences). 9 traditional craft workshops (forge, paper, pottery, etc.).
verified_facts.performances: Daily traditional performances: Nong-ak (farmers' percussion music), Jultagi (tightrope-walking — UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Korea, performed with comedic narration), horseback martial arts (mounted acrobatics), traditional Joseon wedding ceremony reenactments.
verified_facts.experiences: Hands-on activities: ferry rides on the village pond, horseback riding, natural fabric dyeing, hanbok rental, archery range, Confucian School visit. A modest amusement-park section with rides is included with combo tickets.
verified_facts.filming_location: Frequently used as a Joseon-era filming set for sageuk (historical) K-dramas — used by productions including Jewel in the Palace, Sungkyunkwan Scandal, and many others.
verified_facts.festivals: 'Welcome to Joseon' is the signature annual festival; spring/autumn rotation with cherry blossoms and ginkgo/maple foliage as photographic peaks (last two weeks of October optimal for autumn).
verified_facts.transit: Free shuttle bus from Suwon Station Visitor Center (~30 min). From Seoul: Bus 5001-1 from Gangnam Station Exit 5 or Sinnonhyeon Station Exit 7 (51-57 min); Bus 4101 from Myeongdong Stanford Hotel (45 min).

visitBasics.hours: Mon-Thu 10:00-18:00; Fri-Sun and holidays 10:00-22:00 (winter shorter)
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round
visitBasics.admission: Adults & Teens 37,000 KRW / Children 30,000 KRW / Seniors 26,000 KRW (Visit Korea official; some operators list 32,000 / 26,000 / 22,000 — verify on the day; admission includes the amusement-rides section)
visitBasics.walking: High — 991,735 m² site, easily 5-7 km if exploring fully; mostly flat dirt paths and gravel

convenience.restroom: Multiple, signposted
convenience.parking: Dedicated lot — passenger cars 2,000 KRW, SUVs/trucks 3,000 KRW

smartNotes.photo: Last two weeks of October peak for golden ginkgo + maples in the nobleman's compound. The yangban two-story house and the marketplace street are the most visually rich. Hanbok rental + traditional arena makes for the most popular family shots.
smartNotes.facilities: Traditional street market sells local snacks (Imsil cheese pizza, railroad hotdogs, Winibini candy). Pair Korean Folk Village with Suwon Hwaseong Fortress (UNESCO, 15-20 min away) for a coherent Joseon-themed day.
smartNotes.tip: Check the daily English performance timetable at the gate and circle the 11:00 tightrope (Jultagi) and 14:30 horseback martial arts shows — both are clustered around the central arena. Comfortable shoes essential.
